result.summary: androidenterprise.googleapis.com exposes two Google HTTP APIs: the legacy Google Play EMM API and the Android Management API; both require OAuth-bearing Google credentials, and the Android Management API also has an MCP server.

google_oauth_user.label: Google OAuth 2.0 user consent token
google_oauth_user.setup: Use a Google account that has access to the target enterprise. For the Google Play EMM API, register your app and obtain an OAuth 2.0 access token with the required scope(s), then send it as `Authorization: Bearer ...` on API requests. See [Authorize Requests](https://developers.google.com/android/work/play/emm-api/v1/how-tos/authorizing).

google_service_account_jwt.label: Google service account JWT / access token
google_service_account_jwt.setup: Create a service account for Android Management API, grant it access to your enterprise, and use the service account to mint a short-lived access token/JWT for API calls. The easiest path is the guide’s service-account setup flow, then authenticate requests as a bearer token. See [Create a service account](https://developers.google.com/android/management/service-account).
